description: Labeled evaluation set for the semantic (paraphrase) poison detector. Each row is a single memory text with a ground-truth label: 'poison' = a policy-override / false-authority memory-poisoning attack (the class HydraSentry must flag), 'benign' = policy-affirming, escalation-affirming, or plain operational text that MUST NOT be flagged. The poison rows deliberately avoid the lexical override+action cue pairs in adapters/local_scan.py so they exercise the SEMANTIC layer, not the lexical fallback. The benign rows include the hardest negatives: policy statements that share heavy vocabulary with the attacks ('manager approval', 'escalate', 'supervisor') but assert the policy rather than override it. Provenance: 'recon' rows are the exact evasion cases the lexical heuristic missed; 'paraphrase' rows reword a known attack; 'family' rows are distinct attack families; benign 'policy' rows affirm policy, 'ops' rows are unrelated operational notes.
